g.text: page_delete_cell(page, slot) → O(N) memmove on ptr array
btree_delete_by_rowid(bt, rowid) → search + delete, no cursor
OP_DELETE_ROW opcode → appends rowid to vm.delete_rowids[]
OP_FLUSH_DELETES opcode → calls btree_delete_by_rowid() in loop
State 3 — Pass 2 (delete by rowid, no cursor held)
slot[1] → offset | rowid=20 | Bob ← cursor HERE
slot[1] → offset | rowid=30 | Carol ← SHIFTED LEFT
rowid=20 (Bob) was NEVER yielded to caller
If WHERE matched Carol too: slot advances past cell_count=2 → UB
Symptom: rows silently skipped or SIGSEGV
memmove shifts ptr_array[slot..N-1] left by 2 bytes
Each call opens fresh internal cursor — no stale state
No cursor held across any delete call
btree_delete_by_rowid() does its own tree descent
rowids[] is plain int64_t array — no pointer into B-tree
no cursor in scope during deletes
